safety_observation.name: safety_observation
safety_observation.description: A safety observation refers to any recorded note or report about workplace behaviors, conditions, or processes that may impact health and safety, whether positive or negative. In HSE, these are often used to identify trends, reinforce safe practices, or highlight areas needing improvement before incidents occur.
examples: Observed all workers wearing proper PPE in the welding area.
Noted that emergency exits were kept clear during shift.
Identified a missing guard on the conveyor belt during routine check.
Observed forklift operator following all safety protocols.
Reported that spill kits were fully stocked and accessible.

near_miss.name: near_miss
near_miss.description: A near miss is an unplanned event that did not result in injury, illness, or damage but had the potential to do so. In HSE, near misses are critical for proactive risk management, as they highlight underlying hazards or unsafe conditions before they cause harm.
examples: A falling tool narrowly missed a worker passing below.
Forklift reversed into a walkway but stopped before hitting anyone.
Chemical container slipped but was caught before spilling.
Worker tripped over a hose but regained balance without falling.
Overhead load swung out of control but did not strike equipment.

incident.name: incident
incident.description: An incident is any unplanned event that results in or could have resulted in injury, illness, property damage, or environmental harm. In HSE, incidents are formally reported and investigated to determine root causes and prevent recurrence.
examples: Worker sustained a laceration while using a box cutter.
Forklift struck a storage rack causing minor damage.
Oil spill occurred during transfer, requiring cleanup.
Employee slipped on wet floor and sprained ankle.
Fire alarm triggered due to electrical short circuit.

hazard_report.name: hazard_report
hazard_report.description: A hazard report documents the identification of a potential source of harm or unsafe condition in the workplace. In HSE, these reports are used to initiate corrective actions and prevent incidents by addressing risks proactively.
examples: Reported exposed wiring near the loading dock.
Identified unguarded machinery in the maintenance area.
Noted chemical storage area lacking proper signage.
Observed blocked fire extinguisher access.
Reported slippery floor in the break room.

environmental_observation.name: environmental_observation
environmental_observation.description: An environmental observation records any condition, event, or behavior that may impact the environment, such as pollution, waste, or resource use. In HSE, these observations help monitor compliance with environmental standards and identify opportunities for improvement.
examples: Observed oil sheen on surface water near the facility.
Noted improper disposal of chemical containers.
Reported excessive dust emissions from the crusher.
Identified leaking waste drum in storage area.
Observed wildlife entering restricted waste area.

quality_issue.name: quality_issue
quality_issue.description: A quality issue refers to any deviation from established standards or specifications that could affect product integrity, safety, or compliance. In HSE, quality issues are tracked because they may have safety or environmental implications if not addressed.
examples: Detected welds not meeting inspection criteria.
Found packaging with incorrect labeling.
Reported batch of material with off-spec properties.
Identified missing safety data sheets in shipment.
Observed product contamination during filling process.

maintenance_request.name: maintenance_request
maintenance_request.description: A maintenance request is a formal notification to repair, replace, or service equipment, infrastructure, or safety systems. In HSE, timely maintenance is essential to prevent equipment failures that could lead to safety incidents or environmental releases.
examples: Requested repair of faulty emergency lighting.
Submitted work order for leaking pipe in pump room.
Reported malfunctioning fire alarm panel.
Requested replacement of worn conveyor belt.
Notified maintenance of broken safety shower.

unsafe_condition.name: unsafe_condition
unsafe_condition.description: An unsafe condition is any physical state or environmental factor in the workplace that increases the risk of injury, illness, or damage. In HSE, identifying and correcting unsafe conditions is a key preventive measure.
examples: Found unguarded rotating equipment in operation.
Observed water pooling near electrical panels.
Noted missing handrails on stairway.
Identified poor lighting in storage area.
Reported cracked floor tiles in walkway.

unsafe_act.name: unsafe_act
unsafe_act.description: An unsafe act is any behavior or action by a person that increases the likelihood of an accident or injury. In HSE, these are observed and reported to correct risky behaviors and reinforce safe work practices.
examples: Worker bypassed machine guard during operation.
Employee climbed shelving to reach items instead of using a ladder.
Observed worker not wearing required eye protection.
Operator used mobile phone while driving forklift.
Employee ignored lockout/tagout procedure during maintenance.
